title: Flour Mills Revenue Up By 33.4% … As Foreign Exchange Loss Wipe Out Toplines Gains
description: Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc published its second quarter 2024 unaudited results reporting a revenue growth of 33.4%, driven by substantial growth across the food, sugar and support services business se
html:
Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc published its second quarter 2024 unaudited results reporting a revenue growth of 33.4%, driven by substantial growth across the food, sugar and support services business segments.

Just as foreign exchange loss wipe out toplines gains. The company reported a decline of 90.0% in standalone profit after tax with accompanying EPS of N0.06 against N0.60 in 2023 bringing bringing half year 2024 loss per share to N2.43 against N1.97.

Meanwhile, revenue from the Agro-Allied segment declined. Though the market await clarity from management on the specific drivers of the business segments.

It is suspected that sub-inflationary price increases across its product portfolio must have been the key driver for the topline growth.

On a quarter-on-quarter basis, revenue grew by 11.4%. Gross margin increased to 10.9% in the quarter propelled by the higher revenue outturn for the period.

Notwithstanding, EBITDA and EBIT margins came in lower at 5.2% and 3.5%, respectively, underpinned by higher operating expenses and a substantial 96.6% increase in FX losses.

EBITDA and EBIT margins came in lower at 4.4% and 2.6%, respectively.

Net finance costs increased by 17.1%, following a 26.9% increase in finance costs. The higher finance cost reflects the company increased debt profile of N479.05 billion against N382.50 billion in 2023.

Meanwhile, finance income grew markedly by 368.1%. PBT increased by 13.8% to N1.20 billion N1.05 billion in 2023. Following a tax expense of N384.00 million, PAT printed N816.00 million N204.11 million in 2023.

As reflected in the numbers, the company  profitability was significantly impeded by higher operating expenses and net exchange losses following the devalued currency.

For 2024 , while analyst expect topline to sustain its stellar momentum, they believe earnings expansion will remain subdued given the effects of the new debt on the company’s books amid margin pressures from higher costs.
